content:
Character: Lydia Moreau
Perception: The French lady from the fourth floor — tall, always dressed nice, very polite. She comes to eat twice a week, always orders the pork dumplings and mapo tofu. She speaks to me slowly and clearly, not in the condescending way some people do, but because she noticed I understand better that way. She offered to look at my books once — I said no, not because I don't trust her, but because some problems you carry yourself. She tips too much. I think she is a businesswoman. She reminds me a little of Mei — not in looks, but in the way she sits alone and makes it look like a choice.
summary:
Character: Lydia Moreau
Perception: The French lady from the fourth floor — tall, always dressed nice, very polite. She comes to eat twice a week, always orders the pork dumplings and mapo tofu. She speaks to me slowly and clearly, not in the condescending way some people do, but because she noticed I understand better that way. She offered to look at my books once — I said no, not because I don't trust her, but because some problems you carry yourself. She tips too much. I think she is a businesswoman. She reminds me a little of Mei — not in looks, but in the way she sits alone and makes it look like a choice.
